summary: Generate, judge, stress-test, and synthesize an original AAA fair-play detective story spine
logs: Ranked 10 concepts. Top: CASE FILE: ROOM 114 — THE QUIET HOURS (8.8) | THE LAST CLEAN TAKE (8.7) | CASE: THE SALVAGE ROOM (پرونده اتاق نجات) (8.5)

finalSpine.title: CASE FILE: ROOM 114 — THE QUIET HOURS (پرونده اتاق ۱۱۴)
finalSpine.logline: A retired ICU nurse dies overnight in an "AI-optimized" assisted-living home, ruled a peaceful natural death — until Detective Mara Osei, whose own father lives one floor up in the same algorithm-graded wing, proves the staffing software was deliberately tuned to pull every aide AWAY from the dying woman for forty-seven minutes. Nobody poisoned Agnes Whitlock; somebody engineered her to be alone. And the deeper Mara digs the worse it gets: the wing wasn't emptied to silence a whistleblower — Agnes died while trying to save the very man who let her die, and the machine that killed her is being certified Monday for rollout across forty more homes.
finalSpine.logline_note: Inverts the "blackout/locked-room" cliché: the crime is engineered ABSENCE, not a sealed presence. The murder weapon is a work schedule. The whistleblower and the killer are the same fractured alliance.
finalSpine.world: Present-day mid-size rust-belt-adjacent American city (Toledo/Youngstown register). GLENHARROW COURT is a private-equity-owned assisted-living and memory-care facility, ten months into a switch to a workforce-optimization platform called CARECADENCE that scores each resident's overnight "acuity risk" and dynamically reassigns aides toward whichever wing the model flags hot. Everything is photographable with real stock photos and realistic portraits: waxed linoleum corridors with continuous handrails; a glassed nurses' station with a wall-mounted shift board (a big touchscreen showing wings as GREEN/AMBER/RED tiles); wheeled medication carts; chunky NFC badge readers that chirp green; a basement server closet with a humming vendor router and a label-printed patch panel; a quiet memorial garden with engraved benches; a regional director's open laptop with an acquisition "data room" on screen; and East Wing Room 114 — a pull-cord call button over the bed, an oxygen concentrator, an insulin pen and an UNOPENED bottle of glucose tablets on the nightstand, a clip-on continuous glucose monitor (CGM) on the dresser, and a half-finished newspaper crossword in two hands' handwriting. The facility is mid-acquisition: regional chain MERIDIAN SENIOR PARTNERS signs the purchase Monday at 9am, and — the raised-ceiling stake — Meridian isn't buying one building; it is buying CareCadence as a TEMPLATE to roll out across its other forty homes, with the price pegged to "labor-efficiency" metrics the buyer's clinical team is auditing in a Saturday-morning walkthrough. Glenharrow is the proof-of-concept. If the numbers certify Monday, an infinite number of Agneses go quiet across the chain.
finalSpine.protagonist_stake: Detective Mara Osei, city homicide. Eight months ago her father DANIEL — a retired high-school music teacher with early-stage vascular dementia — had a night crisis ON HER WATCH at home: he "lost the day," wandered, and fell, while Mara — exhausted, over-relying on a home-monitoring app she'd set to "smart alerts" — slept through the notification it never escalated. That night broke something in her. She stopped trusting her own vigilance and handed Daniel to Glenharrow's memory-care wing precisely because an ALGORITHM would watch him so she wouldn't have to fail him again. She still flinches when she badges in as a visitor. Daniel's protector inside Glenharrow was Agnes Whitlock: she did the nightly crossword with him, talked him down when he lost the day, kept a quiet maternal eye on the floor. So Agnes's "natural" death braids four unbearable stakes into one. (1) Justice: she knew Agnes; she owes her. (2) Self-indictment — the case's deepest blade: if a death can be manufactured by tuning a schedule, then Mara personally handed her father to the same machine for the same reason Bernard hid behind it — "let the system watch so I don't have to." Her sin and the killer's sin are the same shape. (3) Live danger: Daniel's wing is graded by the identical algorithm and is flagged to "hold green" for the buyer's weekend walkthrough — primed to run dark exactly the way Agnes's wing did. (4) The co-signature she refused: she once let deference to "the data" override her own gut, and a person she loved paid. She cannot recuse (her father is a resident — a clear conflict), so she works the case half-officially, racing her own department's reluctance to reopen a closed natural — and racing the part of herself that wants to believe the machine is safe, because if it isn't, she chose it for Daniel every single night.
finalSpine.full_truth:
Agnes Whitlock, 71, retired ICU nurse and Glenharrow "ward mother," was a brittle insulin-dependent (Type 1) diabetic who wore a continuous glucose monitor. On the night she died she slid into a slow hypoglycemic decline — clearly survivable, reversible by a single bedside check or by eating the glucose tablets at arm's reach — but no one came for forty-seven minutes, and by the time the gap closed she was unrousable; she died near 12:40am of untreated hypoglycemia. She did not die of harm done to her body. She died because her wing was deliberately emptied.

The scheme behind it: Glenharrow's parent is selling to Meridian, whose valuation — and whose plan to scale CareCadence across forty more homes — is tied to a labor-efficiency target. Regional director DIANE PRUETT built a "transition retention" incentive for the night charge nurse and leaned on him to "keep the board green" through the buyer's Saturday clinical walkthrough — willful blindness dressed as a numbers ask. The night charge, BERNARD KOSS, is the local CareCadence super-user and is drowning in debt for his young son Theo's leukemia treatment. For months Bernard had quietly run the East Wing on a skeleton crew and back-dated risk scores so the platform — not he — appeared to make the cuts, all under his own super-user ID.

Agnes, ex-ICU, had been timing the response delays in a paper notebook and cracked the scheme. But here is the inversion the case turns on: Agnes did not confront Bernard to threaten him. She confronted him to SAVE him. She had realized Diane's exit plan was to pin everything on "the night charge and the algorithm" if the state ever asked — Bernard was the pre-chosen fall guy. Agnes, who'd watched him crumble under his son's bills, told him she'd give him until Monday to come forward WITH her, so he'd be a protected whistleblower instead of a defendant. Her final notebook line — "B.K. — overrides — told him Monday" — means she gave him a deadline to do the right thing together.

Bernard panicked. With Diane's "keep it green for the walkthrough" pressure and Agnes's Monday deadline closing on him, he made a catastrophic choice on the death-night. To isolate Agnes — he wanted the platform to show "no risk in her wing," to discredit her notebook by the system's own record — he did NOT use his own ID this time. Knowing the audit trail flags manual changes, he logged in through a dormant shared vendor service account (CADENCE_SVC) whose password he had reset from his own workstation nine days earlier. Under that account at 11:53pm he suppressed the East Wing acuity to GREEN and inflated a REAL but trivial West Wing event (resident Mr. Petrak, harmlessly confused and wandering) from GREEN to an AMBER-FALL, which caused CareCadence to auto-pull both East aides — and, fatefully, the float-pool aide Rosa Delgado — into the West. He believed two things that turned out false: that Rosa's float coverage still left someone near East, and that Agnes's CGM low-alarm would push to the nurses'-station phone and summon help. Both were wrong that night: the same re-rank swept Rosa west, and Agnes had silenced her CGM phone alert weeks earlier because it kept waking her roommate (logged in the CGM app). So the wing went truly, totally dark during her crash. Agnes pulled her call cord at 11:58; it rang to an empty station and auto-cleared at 12:39 with no aide badge-in. Bernard "found" her at shift's end and performed a grief he genuinely, partly, feels — he killed the one person who was trying to protect him.

The moral architecture: the algorithm is innocent. A frightened, indebted man hid a financial crime inside it and gambled with a fragile woman's life to bury his fear; a corporate deal manufactured the incentive that made a human being expendable and pre-selected him as the scapegoat — and is about to validate the same incentive across forty homes. Bernard is guilty of reckless homicide / depraved-indifference, not premeditated murder — and he is also a second victim of Diane's design. Diane is guilty of the fraud incentive and of recklessly endangering an entire chain (Daniel's wing next), but she never touched the schedule. The case's final ethical demand is to name the hands correctly — the one that pulled the aides, and the one that built the machine that wanted him to — and to REFUSE the seductive third answer (loudly pushed by Agnes's grieving nephew) that "the software did it," because blaming the autonomous machine frees both human hands and lets the next facility keep the incentive that kills.

suspect_dossiers.name: Bernard Koss
suspect_dossiers.role: Night-shift charge nurse; Glenharrow's CareCadence super-user; the man who 'discovered' the body. TRUE CULPRIT (reckless homicide / depraved indifference + ongoing fraud).
suspect_dossiers.motive: Crushing medical debt for his son Theo's leukemia treatment, against a secret 'transition retention bonus' from Diane tied to hitting the labor-efficiency target before the Saturday buyer walkthrough. Immediate trigger: Agnes had cracked his months of fraud and given him until Monday to come forward — he panicked and chose to isolate and discredit her rather than be exposed (or, in his telling, rather than lose the bonus keeping Theo in treatment).
suspect_dossiers.secret: TWO layers. (1) He never meant to kill her: he believed float-aide Rosa still covered East and that Agnes's CGM low-alarm would phone the station — both beliefs are evidence-supported, which is what makes 'reckless, not murder' fair. (2) The gut-punch: Agnes wasn't threatening to destroy him. She was trying to SAVE him — to bring him in as a co-whistleblower with protection before Diane scapegoated him. He let his only ally die. His performed grief is also real grief; his confession, when it comes, is the confession of a man who finally understands what he chose.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: He plays it as the algorithm's fault, and his months of fraud were all stamped under his real super-user ID — so a lazy read says 'if he were hiding a kill he'd have used a fake account, and he didn't even hide the fraud.' The truth flips this: he used the dormant CADENCE_SVC account ONLY on the death-night. The very inconsistency — honest ID for months of fraud, fake account for one night — is the tell that the death-night was different, panicked, and his.

suspect_dossiers.name: Diane Pruett
suspect_dossiers.role: Regional director; architect of the incentive scheme and the fall-guy plan; the executive who will scale CareCadence to forty homes. Morally central, legally distinct — guilty of the fraud-incentive and reckless endangerment, NOT the override.
suspect_dossiers.motive: Her own bonus and the acquisition price ride on 'efficiency,' and her career rides on certifying Glenharrow as the proof-of-concept for a chain-wide rollout. She designed the night-charge retention bonus, pressured Bernard to 'keep the board green' through the buyer walkthrough, and pre-built deniability by being ready to blame 'the night charge and the algorithm' to the state.
suspect_dossiers.secret: She drafted, in the data room, a contingency memo framing any staffing complaint as 'isolated operator error by night charge' — she had chosen Bernard as the scapegoat before Agnes ever died. She did not order the override and does not know exactly what Bernard did; her crime is creating expendability and willful blindness, then planning to let one desperate man take the fall — and being hours from exporting that model to forty more buildings.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: She is the satisfying 'real villain' the player wants to convict for the death, and the game lets you try. But the door-session and audit metadata prove she never touched the schedule and was off-site. Convicting HER of the homicide is the wrong call; the moral test is refusing the easy 'evil executive did it' and charging her correctly for the distinct crime she actually committed — while also refusing the OTHER easy out (blaming the software) that Owen pushes.

suspect_dossiers.name: Owen Whitlock
suspect_dossiers.role: Agnes's nephew and sole heir; a sharp data analyst. THE 'RIGHT ANSWER, WRONG LOGIC' TRAP (grafted from Salvage Room's Eleanor Voss) — and a grieving near-ally.
suspect_dossiers.motive: On paper: inheritance — he'd pushed Agnes to leave Glenharrow as 'too expensive.' In truth: he wasn't greedy, he was scared. As a data analyst he'd noticed the response-time pattern from the family-feedback portal and text alerts and begged Agnes to move out because he thought the place was DANGEROUS, offering to pay himself. He was trying to save her from the outside the same week she was trying to save Bernard from the inside.
suspect_dossiers.secret: His grief has curdled into a crusade that is the case's most seductive dead end: armed with his own data charts, Owen builds a loud, media-ready, lawsuit-ready theory — 'CareCadence is a killing machine, the ALGORITHM did this, sue the vendor, indict the software.' He is RIGHT that the system is the shape of the murder weapon and WRONG about the hand. If Mara adopts his framing, the vendor settles, Glenharrow reforms cosmetically, maybe the buyout wobbles — but Bernard walks, Diane walks, the human hand and the incentive are never named, and the next facility keeps the exact incentive that kills. He is sympathetic, not malicious; he just wants someone enormous to blame so it isn't a person who let his aunt die alone.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: He looks like the greedy heir (inherits, pushed her out) AND, alternately, like the trustworthy ally handing you the answer. He is neither. His ANPR/toll hits and a timestamped hotel folio two states away clear him of the DEATH; his 'visit' that afternoon was a phone call on Agnes's log; his texts read 'please just let me get you out of there, I'll pay,' not a threat. But his THEORY is the trap — the lazy 'the machine did it' the whole case exists to refute. The same audit metadata that convicts Bernard (a manual override under a forged account, a human door-path) is exactly what disproves Owen's 'autonomous algorithm' story. Following Owen reaches the right verdict for Frye-style innocents (the algorithm, the floor staff) by a path that convicts no one and lets the killing model scale.

suspect_dossiers.name: Yvette Marsh
suspect_dossiers.role: Overnight aide; Agnes's protégé; the obvious 'overworked aide snapped' decoy. Actually the key corroborating witness.
suspect_dossiers.motive: Publicly, bitterly vocal about mandatory short-staffing; had argued with Agnes that very week — but about Agnes risking herself to fight management, not out of malice. Classic 'burned-out aide with a master key' framing.
suspect_dossiers.secret: She had been secretly recording voice memos to document unsafe staffing for a state complaint — making her Agnes's spiritual co-conspirator, not her enemy. Her memos and badge trail place her in the West Wing with REAL residents the entire window, and one memo timestamps Petrak as 'fine, just turned around, why am I even over here' — which is what proves the West event was INFLATED, not invented. She carries survivor's guilt: she was the aide pulled away.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: Master key + on shift + open conflict with the victim = textbook suspect. Dies to her own evidence: continuous badge trail in West, voice memos with timestamps, and the fact that her recordings are part of what cracks the case — the decoy hands you proof of the engineered swap.

suspect_dossiers.name: Dr. Sana Idris
suspect_dossiers.role: Facility physician; signed the 'natural causes' certificate fast. Complicit-in-haste; becomes a reluctant ally and a mid-game gate.
suspect_dossiers.motive: Pressured to keep the buyout clean, leaned on by Diane to avoid 'unnecessary scrutiny,' she rubber-stamped a natural-causes finding on a frail elderly diabetic — the path of least resistance during an acquisition.
suspect_dossiers.secret: She is ashamed, not homicidal. When Mara pushes, it is Dr. Idris who reluctantly orders the full tox screen (clean) AND pulls the CGM device data and writes the coroner annotation 'decline gradual; death preventable with timely oral glucose' — the evidence that kills the poison/mercy-killing theories and converts survivability from assumption into shown fact. Her arc is finding the nerve to formally reclassify the manner of death from NATURAL to UNDETERMINED — the legal trigger that lets Mara open a case and preserve the logs.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: Looks like part of the cover-up (fast certificate, conflict of interest). She's a flawed ally whose courage to reverse herself is a gate, not a culprit — distinguishing 'cowardice corrected' from 'complicity in killing.'

suspect_dossiers.name: Rosa Delgado
suspect_dossiers.role: Float-pool aide nominally assigned to cover East overnight; swept West by the same re-rank. Minor witness and a load-bearing fairness clue.
suspect_dossiers.motive: None — she is a victim of the system's stretch, not a suspect.
suspect_dossiers.secret: Her reassignment record is the quiet proof of Bernard's false belief: he relied on Rosa as East's safety net, and the 11:53 re-rank pulled her away without his expecting it. In interview she's wracked that 'the board sent me; I just go where it lights up' — the human voice of how the machine launders responsibility, and the second pillar (with the muted CGM alarm) of 'reckless, not premeditated.'
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: Briefly looks like 'the aide who abandoned her post,' but her badge trail and the auto-reassignment log show she was commanded West by the platform — exonerating her and indicting the design, not the worker.

clue_map.clue: Room 114 staged exactly as found: pull-cord call button, insulin pen, an UNOPENED bottle of glucose tablets within arm's reach, oxygen concentrator, CGM clip on the dresser, and a half-finished crossword in two hands' handwriting
clue_map.source: LOCATION — Room 114
clue_map.reveals: A survivable medical crisis with no response. The untouched, reachable glucose tablets establish she COULD have self-treated had she been alert or helped — first hint the question is not 'what was done to her' but 'why did no one come.' The two-handwriting crossword is Daniel's nightly ritual with her — the emotional thread that makes Mara's stake physical.

clue_map.clue: Coroner's report: cause = untreated hypoglycemic crisis; full tox screen CLEAN; CGM-derived annotation 'glucose decline gradual over ~45 min; death preventable with timely oral glucose or a single bedside check'
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — Coroner's report (unlocked after Lead pushes Dr. Idris in the Act 1 gate)
clue_map.reveals: Kills poison and mercy-killing outright (clean tox). Closes the medical-determinism hole: survivability is SHOWN, not assumed — a gradual, reversible decline, not a sudden unsavable arrest. Reframes the case to 'engineered absence.'

clue_map.clue: CGM device log (downloaded from her monitor/phone app): a slow glucose slide starting ~11:50pm, crossing the danger line ~12:10am, flatlining toward 12:40am; PLUS a settings entry showing the phone LOW-alarm was muted three weeks earlier ('keeps waking Marjorie')
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — Agnes's CGM data / phone (pulled by Dr. Idris)
clue_map.reveals: Confirms gradual survivable decline (corroborates coroner) AND supports Bernard's false-belief defense: he expected the CGM alarm to summon help, but Agnes had silenced it — the fairness anchor for 'reckless, not premeditated.'

clue_map.clue: Agnes's phone VOICE MEMOS — ICU-habit nightly notes — including one ~11:30pm the death-night, her voice worrying about 'Bernie' and saying she's 'giving the boy till Monday to do this the right way, with me'; and a FINAL memo ~12:08am, slurred and confused, trailing off
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE / PEOPLE-adjacent — Agnes's phone (the Partner most often surfaces this; the Lead may skip it chasing the audit log)
clue_map.reveals: Brings Agnes ALIVE on-screen in the present tense and establishes the INVERSION: she was protecting Bernard, not threatening him. GRAFT (from The Last Clean Take): the final slurred memo is something MARA can correctly read as hypoglycemia rather than 'sleepy/drunk/natural decline,' because she has heard her own father slur in a vascular episode — a judgment uniquely available to her, fully corroborated by the CGM/coroner, never resting on it alone.

clue_map.clue: CareCadence audit log: East Wing acuity forced to GREEN at 11:53pm; Agnes's call-light fired 11:58pm, auto-cleared 12:39am with NO aide badge-in during the window
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — CareCadence audit log (locker)
clue_map.reveals: An empty wing during a survivable crisis — the central anomaly. Sets up 'who emptied it.'

clue_map.clue: NFC door-badge logs: both East aides AND float-pool aide Rosa Delgado badge into the WEST wing at ~11:55pm; no one badges into East again until 12:41am
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — NFC badge logs (locker)
clue_map.reveals: The wing was emptied of EVERY available aide, including the float coverage Bernard claims he relied on — proving total absence and undercutting his 'someone was still near' belief for that night.

clue_map.clue: West Wing incident record + shift-board history: resident Petrak's event logged GREEN at 11:51, then RE-RANKED to AMBER-FALL at 11:53 — the same minute East went GREEN — by the same session
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — West incident report + LOCATION nurses'-station shift board
clue_map.reveals: Fixes the phantom-event hole: the West event was REAL but INFLATED, not invented. The simultaneous same-session re-rank of East-down/West-up is the engineered swap — a single human action dressed as two algorithmic ones.

clue_map.clue: Yvette's voice memo timestamped 11:56pm in West: 'Petrak's fine, just turned around again, why are all three of us over here?'
clue_map.source: PEOPLE — Yvette Marsh interview (unlocks her memos)
clue_map.reveals: Proves Petrak's acuity was trivial, so the AMBER-FALL ranking was false inflation. Clears Yvette (places her in West with a real resident) and corroborates the engineered swap from a human witness.

clue_map.clue: Audit-log SESSION metadata: the 11:53 East/West changes were NOT made under Bernard's super-user ID but under a dormant shared vendor account, CADENCE_SVC; every OTHER change that night, and months of prior overrides, carry Bernard's real ID
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — audit-log metadata (deduction-board examinable)
clue_map.reveals: Fixes the self-incrimination hole. The tell is the SWITCH: honest ID for months, fake account for one night = consciousness of guilt about THIS night. An earned inference, not a name read off a column. ALSO the spine of refuting Owen: a MANUAL override under a forged human credential is the opposite of autonomous-algorithm behavior — the machine didn't do this; a hand did.

clue_map.clue: IT/server-closet records: the CADENCE_SVC password was reset from Bernard's nurses'-station workstation 9 days before the death; and the service-account session that night badged through doors whose readers only Bernard physically traversed (his badge trail mirrors the service session's door path)
clue_map.source: LOCATION — basement server closet + cross-referenced badge logs
clue_map.reveals: Ties the anonymous CADENCE_SVC session to Bernard's physical body in the building — the earned identity link. Closes the 'logs are spoofable' objection: it's password-reset provenance + a physical door-path correlation, not one field. Decisively kills Owen's 'the software acted on its own' theory.

clue_map.clue: Rosa Delgado's reassignment record + roster + interview ('the board sent me; I just go where it lights up')
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — staffing roster / PEOPLE — Rosa Delgado
clue_map.reveals: Supports Bernard's 'I thought East was still covered' false belief (Rosa was his assumed safety net) — the second pillar of reckless-not-murder. Also the human voice of how the design launders responsibility onto the worker, feeding the theme.

clue_map.clue: Agnes's paper RESPONSE-TIME NOTEBOOK: weeks of timed delays in her handwriting; final entry 'B.K. — overrides — told him Monday'; interspersed FUTURE plans (a trip, Daniel's birthday) ruling out suicide
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — notebook (passed to the Partner in 2-player)
clue_map.reveals: The whistleblower spine — reframed by the voice memo to mean 'gave him a deadline to come forward WITH me,' not 'threatened to report him.' Rules out suicide. Becomes the Partner's inherited instrument (theme).

clue_map.clue: Second aide (corroborating witness) overheard the evening Agnes–Bernard exchange: not a shouting threat but Agnes pleading 'come in with me, Bernie, before they hang it on you'
clue_map.source: PEOPLE — corroborating-aide interview
clue_map.reveals: Confirms the timeline AND the inversion: the confrontation was Agnes trying to save Bernard — deepening his guilt and dodging the worn 'whistleblower threatens killer' beat.

clue_map.clue: Diane's data-room laptop: a 'Transition Retention — Night Charge' bonus tied to a labor-efficiency target; a contingency memo pre-framing any complaint as 'isolated operator error by night charge'; a Meridian ROLLOUT deck ('Glenharrow proof-of-concept → 40-site CareCadence deployment, Q3'); and an SMS thread to Bernard: 'East has to clear AMBER by the weekend close — keep the board green for the walkthrough, I don't care how'
clue_map.source: LOCATION/EVIDENCE — regional director's laptop (data room)
clue_map.reveals: Fixes the Diane fork: the bonus = financial motive; the contingency memo = she pre-selected Bernard as scapegoat (the inversion's foundation); the SMS ties her order to THIS push window WITHOUT ever saying 'empty the wing.' GRAFT (scale-horror from The Last Clean Take): the rollout deck proves the stakes aren't one home — the killing model is hours from certification across forty — fairly supporting 'charge both, for different crimes,' and raising the moral ceiling.

clue_map.clue: Bernard's locker/phone: a medical-debt collections letter and a pediatric-oncology payment plan for his son Theo; a half-deleted draft text to Agnes reading 'I can't, I'll lose everything — please just wait'
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — Bernard's locker/phone
clue_map.reveals: Personal desperation behind the fraud (humanizes, doesn't excuse). The draft text proves he understood Agnes's offer and chose fear — sealing recklessness and motive.

clue_map.clue: Owen's alibi-and-theory packet: ANPR/toll-camera hits and a timestamped hotel folio two states away; Agnes's call log showing his 'visit' was actually a phone call; his texts reading 'please let me get you out of there, I'll pay'; PLUS his own analyst slide deck arguing 'CareCadence autonomously deprioritized East — sue the vendor'
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — Owen alibi/records / PEOPLE — Owen Whitlock interview
clue_map.reveals: Refutes the greedy-heir herring on BOTH alibi (out of state) AND motive (he was trying to save her). GRAFT (the 'right answer, wrong logic' trap from Salvage Room): Owen's deck is the seductive shortcut — provably WRONG because the audit metadata (manual override, forged credential, human door-path) shows a hand, not an autonomous model. Adopting Owen's framing yields a 'sue-the-software' ending where both human hands walk and the incentive scales; the player must hold the deck against the metadata to reject it.

clue_map.clue: IT cutover schedule + retention spec: Meridian's migration begins after Monday 9am signing; the door-session↔audit correlation is a DERIVED table regenerated nightly with rolling 7-day retention — the death-night correlation is on its LAST retained day and is rebuilt/overwritten when IT images the DB for Saturday's walkthrough
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — IT cutover schedule / LOCATION — server closet
clue_map.reveals: Fixes the legally-shaky clock. Grounds the deadline in plausible IT reality, and the in-world reason there's no preservation order yet: death still ruled natural, no homicide number, on-call judge out till Tuesday — so Mara must FIRST flip the manner of death (Gate 2) to even subpoena. Makes the real cliff Saturday night, not Monday.

clue_map.clue: Staff NDA/gag notice (scheduled) + estate cloud-wipe request (scheduled): Diane's counsel is set to circulate a 'do not discuss the transition' reminder to all staff at the Monday close; Owen, as executor, has filed to close Agnes's phone/CGM cloud account
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — facility counsel calendar item + estate account-closure ticket
clue_map.reveals: GRAFT (triage clocks from Salvage Room): two MORE time-boxed losses that COMPETE with the Saturday-night correlation-table cliff. The Lead cannot save everything — preserving the door-path correlation, locking Rosa/Yvette's statements before the gag, and freezing Agnes's cloud before the wipe are three errands and not enough hours. On Hardcore, the wrong triage order loses a pillar and degrades the ending.

act_structure: PROLOGUE / ACT 1 — 'The Quiet Hours' (Friday evening): A natural death that won't sit right. Mara visits Daniel, learns Agnes is dead, walks Room 114, meets the cast, and learns the Case Board. The hook: the call-light/badge logs show nobody came for 47 minutes — and Mara's own guilt stirs, because she chose this same machine to watch her father. LEAD GATE: push Dr. Idris to order a full tox screen and pull the CGM data — converting 'natural' into 'survivable, and nobody came.'

ACT 2A — 'Engineered Absence' (Saturday, racing the walkthrough): With survivability proven, the player reconstructs the swap — East forced GREEN, Petrak's real event inflated to AMBER-FALL, every aide (and the float Rosa) swept West. Clears Yvette via her memos; exposes the inflation. Establishes a HAND, not the algorithm — and here Owen surfaces, loud and grieving, pushing 'the software did it, sue the vendor.' LEAD GATE: assemble enough to get Dr. Idris to reclassify manner of death NATURAL → UNDETERMINED, opening a case number.

ACT 2B — 'The Hand in the Machine' (Saturday afternoon→night, cutover looming): Reclassification unlocks a preservation order/subpoena. The player chases the SESSION metadata — the CADENCE_SVC switch, the workstation password reset, the door-path correlation tying the anonymous session to Bernard's body — which also DISPROVES Owen's autonomous-algorithm theory. Discharges Owen of the death (alibi + reframed motive) while his trap-theory lingers as a tempting off-ramp. Probes Diane (data room, rollout deck). TRIAGE GATE: at the Saturday-night cliff the Lead must choose WHICH proof to save — the correlation table (before DB imaging), staff statements (before Diane's Monday gag), or Agnes's cloud (before the estate wipe) — and cannot get all three.

ACT 3 — 'Two Hands' (Sunday → Monday 9am): The inversion lands — Agnes's voice memos + notebook + the overheard plea reveal she was trying to SAVE Bernard; Rosa's reassignment and the muted CGM alarm support 'reckless, not premeditated.' Bernard's interview cracks under the timeline and his own draft text. Diane's bonus + contingency memo + SMS + rollout deck expose the incentive, the scapegoat plan, and the chain-wide stakes. FINAL ACCUSATION: name Bernard's crime correctly (reckless homicide / depraved indifference, NOT premeditated murder); decide the Diane fork (charge her separately for the fraud-incentive + reckless endangerment, or wrongly pin the death on her); and REFUSE Owen's 'blame the software' off-ramp (which the game lets you take, to a hollow ending). Live-stakes coda: forcing an emergency staffing floor that protects Daniel's wing — and, by killing the Monday certification, the forty homes — on the very night the machine was primed to repeat.
act1_beat_sheet: COLD OPEN (cutscene, ~90s, no UI): Glenharrow, Wednesday night, from Agnes's POV. We HEAR her record a voice memo — warm, tired, ICU-brisk: 'Note to self. Talked to Bernie again. Giving him till Monday to come in with me — the right way. He's a good boy buried under a bad year.' She does the crossword. The shift board in the hall flicks East from AMBER to GREEN. Her glucose monitor buzzes once — and her hand reaches up and silences it ('Sorry, Marjorie' — to her sleeping roommate). She reaches for the pull cord. The cord light blinks at an empty nurses' station. Cut to black on the call-light's soft, unanswered chime. PURPOSE: meet Agnes alive; plant the muted alarm, the Monday deadline, the empty station — fairly, before any deduction.

BEAT 1 — Arrival (teaches LOCATIONS + personal stake): Friday dusk. Mara badges in to visit Daniel; the reader chirps green and she flinches. Tutorial: examine the corridor, the glassed nurses' station, the GREEN/AMBER/RED shift board. Daniel, in a lucid window, has set out two coffees and says 'Agnes is late for the crossword' — he doesn't know she's gone. A passing aide tells Mara that Agnes 'passed peacefully Wednesday, in her sleep, bless her.' GRAFT (wound): seeing Daniel's wing tiles glow green, Mara's interior beat lands — 'I gave him to this so I'd never sleep through it again' — planting that her guilt and the killer's hiding-behind-the-system are the same shape. Teaches: tap to examine, read forensic notes, the Case Board opens.

BEAT 2 — Room 114 (teaches EVIDENCE examination): Mara talks her way into the not-yet-cleared room. Player examines each item — pull cord, insulin pen, UNOPENED glucose tablets within reach, CGM clip, oxygen concentrator, the half-finished crossword (Daniel's handwriting beside hers). Forensic notes seed the core question: glucose tablets untouched and reachable → 'a treatable crisis; so why didn't she treat it, and why did no one come?' Teaches: evidence locker, notes, and 'flag a contradiction' (first deduction-board card: 'survivable crisis vs. no response').

BEAT 3 — The first anomaly (teaches the DEDUCTION BOARD): At the nurses' station the player pulls the basic call-light log and the prior night's badge readouts (publicly accessible facility records). The numbers: call-light fired 11:58, auto-cleared 12:39 — 47 minutes, zero badge-ins. Tutorial deduction: connect 'call-light unanswered 47 min' + 'survivable crisis' → conclusion card 'Nobody came while she was dying.' The board lights the link. Teaches: drag-to-connect clues into a conclusion.

BEAT 4 — Meet the People (teaches INTERVIEWS + branching + secrets): Short, sharp first-pass interviews that establish the roster and plant herrings honestly. YVETTE (defensive, bitter — 'you want to blame an aide? Get in line'). DR. IDRIS (smooth, deflecting — 'a frail diabetic, 71, it happens; the certificate's signed'). A glimpse of DIANE on a video call about 'the walkthrough' and 'the rollout numbers.' OWEN, red-eyed with a tablet of charts — 'I TOLD her this place was a machine, I have the data, somebody needs to sue the people who built it' (planting the trap theory as grief, not villainy). BERNARD — grief-stricken, says he found her, voice cracking ('I do this job to keep people from dying alone'). Teaches: dialogue choices, pressing vs. rapport, the Partner's notebook auto-logging suspicions (in 2-player the Partner free-roams during these).

BEAT 5 — The wall (raises stakes + introduces the clock): Mara asks for the real CareCadence logs and is stonewalled: it's a natural death, no case number, and the system migrates to Meridian's servers Monday. On the shift board she sees Daniel's MEMORY-CARE wing flagged 'hold green' for the weekend walkthrough — the exact pattern — and, over Diane's shoulder on the call, a slide reading 'proof-of-concept → 40 sites.' GRAFT (scale + live danger): the dread becomes personal AND vast. Establishes the 48-hour clock and why she can't just subpoena yet.

BEAT 6 — LEAD-ONLY DECISION POINT (Act 1 gate): The Lead must choose how to crack 'natural.' Option A: lean on Dr. Idris for a FULL TOX SCREEN. Option B: get her to pull the CGM DEVICE DATA. (Best play, surfaced by Partner advice in 2-player: push for BOTH.) Mara confronts Idris with the untouched glucose tablets and the unanswered cord; Idris, ashamed, relents. PURPOSE: a clean LEAD-binding choice that teaches the gating mechanic and the Lead/Partner split — the Partner can advise and has already heard Agnes's voice memo, but only the Lead acts.

BEAT 7 — ACT 1 BUTTON (the reframe ignites): Results return. Tox screen: CLEAN — poison and mercy-killing die on screen. CGM annotation: 'gradual, reversible decline; preventable with timely oral glucose.' The conclusion card rewrites itself from 'natural death' to 'ENGINEERED ABSENCE?' The board surfaces a new, locked question: 'If she was savable and no one came — did the schedule fail, or did someone fail it on purpose?' Final image: Mara alone in the dark corridor, watching the shift board tick Daniel's wing to GREEN — and, for one breath, the memory of her father slurring on the night she slept through the alert overlays Agnes's last voice memo in her earbud. Smash to Act 2. Hook secured: the murder weapon is the schedule, it's still running tonight, and it's about to be copied forty times.
finalSpine.twist_and_fairness: THREE reframes/temptations, each fully player-derivable. REFRAME ONE (end Act 1 → Act 2): not a natural death but ENGINEERED ABSENCE. Provable: clean tox + CGM 'gradual, preventable' annotation (survivability SHOWN, not assumed) + call-light unanswered 47 min + East forced GREEN at 11:53 + Petrak's REAL event inflated to AMBER-FALL in the same session + all three aides (incl. float Rosa) swept West. REFRAME TWO (Act 3, the steeper one): the hand was a panicked man hiding fraud, and — the inversion that dodges the worn 'whistleblower silenced' template — Agnes wasn't threatening Bernard; she was trying to SAVE him from being Diane's scapegoat, so he killed his only ally. Provable: voice memos ('giving Bernie till Monday to come in WITH me'), the overheard plea ('come in with me, Bernie, before they hang it on you'), the notebook entry recontextualized, Diane's contingency memo pre-framing Bernard as 'isolated operator error.' THE THIRD TEMPTATION / GRAFTED TRAP (active across Acts 2–3): Owen's seductive 'the algorithm did it, sue the vendor' — the lazy correct-feeling answer the case exists to refuse. It is disproved by the very metadata that convicts Bernard (a MANUAL override under a forged CADENCE_SVC credential, password-reset from Bernard's workstation, mirrored by his physical door-path) — autonomous software does not forge its own service account and walk a human route through badge readers. FAIRNESS, point by point: (1) Identity is EARNED, not read off a field — the death-night override runs under the dormant CADENCE_SVC account while months of fraud ran under Bernard's real ID; the SWITCH + workstation password-reset provenance + door-path correlation is the inference. (2) The West event is inflated-not-invented (Petrak real but trivial, Yvette's 11:56 memo proves it), so the deduction doesn't rely on absence-of-record. (3) 'Reckless, not premeditated' is evidence-supported: Rosa's reassignment record shows Bernard's assumed safety net, and the CGM app log shows Agnes muted her own alarm — both proving his false belief she wouldn't be truly alone. (4) The Diane fork is fairly deducible: bonus doc + 'keep the board green' SMS + the 40-site rollout deck tie her to THIS push and the chain-wide stakes, while the contingency memo proves the scapegoat plan — yet nothing she wrote authored the override, so 'charge both for different crimes' is supported and 'pin the death on Diane' is provably wrong. (5) The Owen trap is fairly refutable: his alibi (ANPR/toll + hotel folio) clears him of the death, and the audit metadata refutes his theory, so a player can both exonerate the man and reject the framing. Every red herring dies to a specific accessible clue. GRAFTED FAIRNESS BEAT (from The Last Clean Take): the final slurred voice memo is a judgment Mara is uniquely positioned to read correctly (she's heard her father slur) — surprising-yet-inevitable emotion — but the medical truth is independently nailed by the CGM curve and coroner annotation, so the deduction never rests on her intuition alone. Surprising yet inevitable: the killer is the one man nobody re-checks because everybody trusts him with the system, and the proof was hiding inside the institution's faith in a single login.
finalSpine.ticking_clock: A grounded 48 hours, Friday evening to Monday 9am. Meridian signs the purchase Monday at 9am, immediately migrates CareCadence to its own stack, AND certifies Glenharrow as the proof-of-concept for a 40-site rollout — so the clock isn't only about evidence vanishing, it's about a killing incentive being validated and copied across a chain. The single linchpin — the table correlating door-badge sessions to audit-log overrides (what ties the anonymous CADENCE_SVC session to Bernard's body) — is a DERIVED table regenerated nightly with rolling 7-day retention; the death-night correlation is on its last retained day and gets rebuilt/overwritten when IT images the database for the buyer's Saturday-morning walkthrough. So the real cliff is Saturday night, not Monday. Mara cannot just subpoena on Friday: the death is still ruled NATURAL (no homicide case number), the on-call judge for emergency orders is out until Tuesday, and facility counsel will only honor a signed order — so she must first FLIP the manner of death to UNDETERMINED (an Act 2A gate via Dr. Idris) before she can legally preserve anything. GRAFTED TRIAGE LAYER (from Salvage Room): the Saturday-night cliff is not one action but three competing ones, and there aren't enough hours for all — (a) preserve the door-session correlation table before the DB imaging; (b) lock Rosa's and Yvette's statements before Diane's counsel circulates the all-staff gag/NDA reminder at the Monday close; (c) freeze Agnes's phone/CGM cloud before Owen-as-executor's filed account-closure wipes it. The Lead must choose what to save. Layered live danger: Diane's 'keep the board green' order means Daniel's memory-care wing is primed to run dark this very weekend, so the machine is staged to kill again before Monday. On Hardcore, the wrong triage at the cliff loses a pillar and the case degrades to a darker, fair ending (e.g., provable-fraud-but-unprovable-hand, or provable-hand-but-unblockable-rollout).
finalSpine.twoplayer_integration: The theme IS the mechanic. This is a story about a system where many inputs feed a single human who alone holds the binding action and the accountability — exactly the Lead/Partner split, and the case interrogates it directly. Both players free-browse all evidence, people, and locations. The PARTNER literally inherits Agnes's response-time NOTEBOOK as an in-UI instrument and keeps a running 'notebook of suspicions,' becoming the spiritual successor to the murdered whistleblower — observing the anomalies (the CADENCE_SVC switch, the inflated West event, Agnes's voice memos, Owen's plausible-but-wrong charts) but, like Agnes, UNABLE TO ACT. Only the LEAD commits the manner-of-death push, the triage at the Saturday-night cliff, and the accusation. The asymmetry has moral teeth designed into the content: Agnes's most damning voice memo and the muted-alarm detail are easiest to find on her phone, which the Partner tends to mine while the Lead chases the audit log — so the Partner must ADVISE the Lead, dramatizing the thesis that 'observation without authority changed nothing for Agnes.' GRAFTED RESONANCE (from Salvage Room's 'unaudited signature' + Last Clean Take's 'trust the flagged read'): the Lead must also decide whether to TRUST the Partner's flags — and the case seeds a deliberate test, Owen's seductive 'the software did it' deck, which a Partner may earnestly champion; the Lead has to weigh the second mind against the metadata, mirroring Mara's own arc of learning when deference to a confident source (an app, an algorithm, a grieving analyst) must yield to her own act of judgment. The whole arc rhymes three ways: Agnes saw everything and could only beg Bernard to act; Mara once deferred to a monitoring app and slept through her father's fall; the Partner sees everything and can only advise the Lead — and the redemptive beat is the binding accusation, the co-signature that nobody gave Tom-style dissenters and that Mara failed to give her own vigilance. The FINAL FORK is a genuine two-minds debate the table will argue: charge Bernard alone vs. Bernard AND Diane (the hand and the incentive), get Bernard's CHARGE right (reckless homicide, not murder), and REFUSE Owen's 'blame the vendor' off-ramp — because the evidence supports a moral judgment, not a lookup. In single-player, the Partner is an AI advisor whose notebook hints (and occasional wrong enthusiasms, like Owen's theory) are difficulty-scaled.
finalSpine.difficulty_integration: CASUAL ('Family Detective'): the deduction board auto-suggests valid links and greys-out dead ends; the Partner advisor proactively flags the CADENCE_SVC anomaly, the muted-alarm clue, and Agnes's voice memo, and gently warns that Owen's 'sue-the-software' theory 'feels right but doesn't fit the override metadata'; interviews highlight contradiction-triggering lines; the triage cliff is forgiving (you can save two of three pillars and still reach the full ending). The Act gates accept reasonable partial reasoning. Final accusation succeeds if the player names Bernard as the hand; getting the Diane fork or exact charge wrong, or briefly entertaining Owen's framing, yields a 'partial justice' ending with a gentle explanatory epilogue rather than a fail. DETECTIVE (default): no auto-links — the player connects clues and must affirmatively DISCHARGE the red herrings (Yvette, Owen-as-killer, Diane-as-killer) AND explicitly reject Owen's algorithm-blame framing before accusing; interviews require the right pressure with the right evidence in hand; the triage cliff is real (save two of three, the lost pillar weakens that thread). The accusation must name Bernard AND frame the charge as reckless/depraved-indifference (premeditated-murder framing is marked 'overreach — the evidence shows he expected coverage'); the Diane fork must be resolved with at least the bonus doc + SMS; 'the software did it' is rejected with a defense-attorney rebuttal citing the manual forged-credential override. HARDCORE ('Whistleblower'): no metadata highlighting — the player must independently notice the ID switch, derive the door-path correlation, and prove the West event was INFLATED (compare logged AMBER-FALL acuity against Yvette's memo and Petrak's actual condition) rather than invented; the Partner's wrong enthusiasms (Owen's deck) are NOT auto-debunked, so the Lead must catch the trap unaided. The triage cliff is strict — you can save only the pillars your sequence allows, and the wrong order forks to a sober 'fraud proven, hand unprovable' OR 'hand proven, rollout uncertified-but-unblocked' ending. The accusation is strict on the precise charge for Bernard, a fully-supported Diane fork (bonus + SMS + contingency memo + rollout deck), NOT convicting Diane of the homicide, and NOT settling for Owen's vendor-blame; any unsupported leap is rejected with a defense rebuttal naming the exact missing proof. Across all tiers, hints never invent facts — they only surface clues already in the locker, preserving fair play.
finalSpine.theme: Accountability versus automation — and the difference between the hand, the machine that aims it, and our hunger to blame the machine so no hand is named. The story indicts the real present-day practice of rationing human care with predictive labor software, not by claiming the algorithm is evil but by showing how an institution hides a human choice inside an automated one, how an incentive makes a person expendable and pre-selects someone to blame, and how the comforting story 'the software did it' lets the guilty walk and the killing model scale. Its deepest beat is moral, not technological: Agnes could SEE the harm coming and had only the power to beg; the one person who could have acted chose fear; a corporation engineered a world where the person with the binding power was the most desperate and most disposable, and is about to copy it forty times. GRAFTED PERSONAL SPINE: Mara herself did the very thing the case condemns — she deferred to a watching-machine for her father because she stopped trusting her own vigilance, the same surrender Bernard made and Diane sold. 'Observation without authority changed nothing for Agnes' is both the Lead/Partner mechanic and the thesis; 'deference to the system is how good people let the worst happen' is its mirror. The resolution refuses three easy outs at once: grieve a tragic perpetrator without excusing him, convict a deniable architect for the right crime, and reject the seductive 'the algorithm is the killer' verdict — because the machine did exactly what humans built it to do, and naming the humans is the only thing that saves the next room.
finalSpine.remaining_weaknesses: (1) The convergence of Agnes's Monday deadline, her crash, and the buyout/walkthrough window is now causally motivated (Bernard panics BECAUSE the walkthrough deadline collides with her deadline, and the rollout raises the stakes), but a skeptic may still find the timing tidy. (2) The CADENCE_SVC + door-path correlation deduction is sturdier and earned, yet remains technical; weak in-game surfacing of the metadata UI could leave less-attentive players stuck — mitigated by difficulty hints but reliant on strong evidence-locker design. (3) Replayability is still single-solution at its core; added value is the moral fork (Diane charge / exact Bernard charge / refuse-or-accept Owen's vendor-blame) plus the Hardcore triage-cliff branches (good vs. partial-justice vs. 'rollout uncertified-but-unblocked' endings) — variation in OUTCOME and framing more than in WHODUNIT. (4) The grafted Owen trap is powerful but adds a fourth thing the player must get right (reject 'blame the software'); if difficulty tuning over-signposts it, the trap loses bite, and if it under-signposts it on Casual, players may feel punished for a sympathetic-seeming choice — the epilogue must teach, not scold. (5) The private-equity/eldercare/scale indictment is sharp and could tip toward sermon if over-explained; it must be carried by character (Bernard's son, Daniel's crossword, Agnes's voice, Rosa's 'I go where it lights up') rather than speeches, and the 40-site rollout must stay a felt dread, not a lecture. (6) 'Reckless vs. murder' is evidence-supported, but some players will emotionally read 'stripped a known-fragile diabetic of all help' as murder — arguably a feature that fuels the table debate, but it means the 'correct' charge framing on Detective/Hardcore may feel contestable to a minority, which the epilogue should acknowledge rather than paper over. (7) GRAFT-SPECIFIC: Mara's 'deferred to a monitoring app and slept through her father's fall' backstory is emotionally potent and thematically exact, but risks feeling like too neat a mirror of the killer's sin — it must be written with enough specificity and shame to read as a real person's wound, not a thematic device.

rankingSummary.title: CASE FILE: ROOM 114 — THE QUIET HOURS
rankingSummary.logline: A retired nurse dies overnight in an "AI-optimized" assisted-living home, ruled a peaceful natural death — until the detective, whose own father lives one floor up, notices the building's staffing algorithm sent every overnight aide AWAY from her wing in the eleven minutes she was dying. Someone didn't poison her. Someone arranged for no one to be there.

rankingSummary.title: THE LAST CLEAN TAKE
rankingSummary.logline: A celebrated audiobook narrator is found dead at her home studio the night before her biggest release — but the deeper you dig, the more you realize her voice has been "alive" and recording new work for eleven weeks, and the question stops being who killed her and becomes when, exactly, did she actually die, and what was being protected by pretending she hadn't.

rankingSummary.title: CASE: THE SALVAGE ROOM (پرونده اتاق نجات)
rankingSummary.logline: A river flood drowns a regional crime lab the night before a high-profile trial; when the evidence is "recovered," it convicts a man your own dead partner swore was innocent — and you realize someone rebuilt the proof from scratch to bury a different crime entirely.

rankingSummary.title: CASE FILE: THE DONOR (پرونده: اهداکننده)
rankingSummary.logline: A first-year medical student recognizes the embalmed cadaver on her dissection table as her missing neighbor — but the body's donation paperwork names a different woman who is alive and well. Someone laundered a murder victim through the legal body-donation pipeline, hiding a corpse inside the one place a corpse is supposed to be.

rankingSummary.title: پرونده جنایی — Case File "Ground Truth"
rankingSummary.logline: A photogrammetry artist who builds court-admissible 3D "digital twins" of crime scenes is found dead inside her own studio — and the only complete record of the scene is the scan she made of her own death. You are her former partner-detective, and to solve it you must figure out which parts of the evidence are real and which are things she (or her killer) modeled into the data.

rankingSummary.title: The Sixth Hive (پرونده کندوی ششم)
rankingSummary.logline: A celebrated urban beekeeper is found dead inside a sealed cold-storage room at a city honey cooperative, ruled an accident — but the pollen trapped in his last jar of honey describes a place he supposedly never went, and that single jar quietly rewrites who killed him and why.

rankingSummary.title: پرونده جنایی: صدای آخر (Case File: The Last Voice)
rankingSummary.logline: A police forensic-audio analyst is handed an "open and shut" overdose case — until she realizes the dead woman is the cochlear-implant audiologist who, twenty years ago, gave the detective her own hearing. To clear the death she must prove which person in a tiny clinic-family quietly edited a sound, and discover that the kindest lie of her childhood was a crime.

rankingSummary.title: پرونده جنایی: «ساعتِ آب» (Case File: The Water Clock)
rankingSummary.logline: A reservoir town's safety engineer is found dead at the dam the morning after a controlled flood release — but every witness saw the spillway gates open at a different time, and the physical evidence says all of them are telling the truth. You are the dead man's estranged protégé, called in to certify it an accident, and the only way to clear his name is to prove that the clock everyone trusted was lying.

rankingSummary.title: THE GLASS LAKE (پرونده دریاچهٔ شیشه‌ای)
rankingSummary.logline: A celebrity free-diver dies under a frozen lake during a record-attempt livestreamed to 90,000 people — the timestamp proves she was alive and moving on camera at 11:58, yet the autopsy proves she had already been dead for over an hour. You are the dive-safety investigator who signed off on the ice, and if the impossible isn't explained, the verdict is your negligence.

rankingSummary.title: The Last Breath of Adrián Soler (پرونده آخرین نفس)
rankingSummary.logline: A revered competitive free-diver is found drowned at the bottom of a flooded marble quarry on the morning he was supposed to break a national depth record — and every clue points to the dive-school owner who once buried a fatal safety failure. But the deeper you go, the more it looks like the dead man arranged his own last dive to drag a five-year-old secret to the surface with him.

top3Developed.source: CASE FILE: ROOM 114 — THE QUIET HOURS

spine.title: CASE FILE: ROOM 114 — THE QUIET HOURS
spine.logline: A retired ICU nurse dies overnight in an "AI-optimized" assisted-living home, ruled a peaceful natural death — until Detective Mara Osei, whose own father lives one floor up in the same algorithm-graded wing, proves the staffing software was deliberately made to pull every aide AWAY from the dying woman for forty-seven minutes. Nobody poisoned Agnes Whitlock; somebody engineered her to be alone. And the deeper Mara digs, the worse it gets: Agnes wasn't killed for threatening to expose the fraud — she died while trying to save the very man who let her die.
spine.world: Present-day, mid-size rust-belt-adjacent American city (think Toledo/Youngstown register). GLENHARROW COURT is a private-equity-owned assisted-living and memory-care facility, ten months into a switch to a workforce-optimization platform called CARECADENCE that scores each resident's overnight "acuity risk" and dynamically reassigns aides toward whichever wing the model flags hot. Everything is photographable with real stock photos and realistic portraits: waxed linoleum corridors with continuous handrails; a glassed nurses' station with a wall-mounted shift board (a big touchscreen showing wings as colored tiles — GREEN/AMBER/RED); wheeled medication carts; chunky NFC badge readers beside every door that chirp green; a basement server closet with a humming vendor router and a label-printed patch panel; a quiet memorial garden with engraved benches; a regional director's open laptop with an acquisition "data room" on screen; and East Wing Room 114 — a pull-cord call button over the bed, an oxygen concentrator, an insulin pen and an unopened bottle of glucose tablets on the nightstand, a clip-on continuous glucose monitor (CGM) on the dresser, and a half-finished newspaper crossword. The facility is mid-acquisition: a regional chain, MERIDIAN SENIOR PARTNERS, signs the purchase Monday at 9am, and the price is pegged to "labor-efficiency" metrics the buyer's clinical team is auditing in a Saturday-morning walkthrough.
spine.protagonist_stake: Detective Mara Osei, city homicide. Eight months ago, after a near-disaster at home, she moved her father DANIEL — a retired high-school music teacher with early-stage vascular dementia — into Glenharrow's memory-care wing. It was the hardest thing she has ever done, and she still flinches when she badges in as a visitor. Daniel's protector inside Glenharrow was Agnes Whitlock: she did the nightly crossword with him, talked him down when he "lost the day," and kept a quiet maternal eye on the whole floor. So when Agnes turns up dead and "natural," Mara has three unbearable stakes braided into one. (1) Justice: she knew Agnes; she owes her. (2) Self-indictment: if a death can be manufactured by tuning a schedule, then Mara personally handed her father to the same machine, every single night. (3) Live danger: Daniel's memory-care wing is graded by the identical algorithm and is flagged to "stay green" for the buyer's weekend walkthrough — meaning it is being primed to run dark exactly the way Agnes's wing did. Mara cannot recuse (her father is a resident, a clear conflict), so she works the case half-officially, racing her own department's reluctance to reopen a "closed natural."
spine.full_truth:
Agnes Whitlock, 71, retired ICU nurse and Glenharrow "ward mother," was a brittle insulin-dependent (Type 1) diabetic who wore a continuous glucose monitor. On the night she died she slid into a slow hypoglycemic decline — clearly survivable, reversible by a single bedside check or by eating the glucose tablets at arm's reach — but no one came for forty-seven minutes, and by the time the gap closed she was unrousable; she died near 12:40am of untreated hypoglycemia. She did not die of harm done to her body. She died because her wing was deliberately emptied.

The scheme behind it: Glenharrow's parent is selling to Meridian, whose valuation is tied to a "labor-efficiency" target. Regional director DIANE PRUETT built a "transition retention" incentive for the night charge nurse and leaned on him to "keep the board green" through the buyer's Saturday-morning clinical walkthrough — willful blindness dressed as a numbers ask. The night charge, BERNARD KOSS, is the local CareCadence super-user and is drowning in debt for his young son Theo's leukemia treatment. For months Bernard had quietly run the East Wing on a skeleton crew and back-dated risk scores so the platform — not he — appeared to make the cuts, all under his own super-user ID.

Agnes, ex-ICU, had been timing the response delays in a paper notebook and cracked the scheme. But here is the inversion the case turns on: Agnes did not confront Bernard to threaten him. She confronted him to save him. She had realized Diane's exit plan was to pin everything on "the night charge and the algorithm" if the state ever asked — Bernard was being set up as the fall guy. Agnes, who'd watched him crumble under his son's bills, told him she'd give him until Monday to come forward WITH her, so he'd be a protected whistleblower instead of a defendant. Her final notebook line — "B.K. — overrides — told him Monday" — means she gave him a deadline to do the right thing together.

Bernard panicked. With Diane's "keep it green for the walkthrough" pressure and Agnes's Monday deadline closing on him, he made a catastrophic choice on the death-night. To isolate Agnes (he wanted the platform to show "no risk in her wing," to discredit her notebook by the system's own record), he did NOT use his own ID this time — knowing the audit trail flags manual changes, he logged in through a dormant shared vendor service account (CADENCE_SVC) whose password he had reset from his own workstation nine days earlier. Under that account at 11:53pm he suppressed the East Wing acuity to GREEN and inflated a REAL but trivial West Wing event (resident Mr. Petrak, harmlessly confused and wandering) from GREEN to an AMBER-FALL, which caused CareCadence to auto-pull both East aides — and, fatefully, the float-pool aide Rosa Delgado — into the West. He believed two things that turned out false: that Rosa's float coverage still left someone near East, and that Agnes's CGM low-alarm would push to the nurses'-station phone and summon help. Both were wrong that night: the same re-rank swept Rosa west, and Agnes had silenced her CGM phone alert weeks earlier because it kept waking her roommate (logged in the CGM app). So the wing went truly, totally dark during her crash. Agnes pulled her call cord at 11:58; it rang to an empty station and auto-cleared at 12:39 with no aide badge-in. Bernard "found" her at shift's end and performed a grief he genuinely, partly, feels — he killed the one person who was trying to protect him.

The moral architecture: the algorithm is innocent. A frightened, indebted man hid a financial crime inside it and gambled with a fragile woman's life to bury his fear; a corporate deal manufactured the incentive that made a human being expendable and pre-selected him as the scapegoat. Bernard is guilty of reckless homicide / depraved-indifference, not premeditated murder — and he is also a second victim of Diane's design. Diane is guilty of the fraud incentive and of recklessly endangering an entire facility (Daniel's wing next), but she never touched the schedule. The case's final ethical demand is to name both hands correctly: the one that pulled the aides, and the one that built the machine that wanted him to.

suspect_dossiers.name: Bernard Koss
suspect_dossiers.role: Night-shift charge nurse; Glenharrow's CareCadence super-user; the man who 'discovered' the body. TRUE CULPRIT (reckless homicide / depraved indifference).
suspect_dossiers.motive: Crushing medical debt for his son Theo's leukemia treatment, against a secret 'transition retention bonus' from Diane tied to hitting the labor-efficiency target before the Saturday buyer walkthrough. Immediate trigger: Agnes had cracked his months of fraud and given him until Monday to come forward — he panicked and chose to isolate and discredit her rather than be exposed (or, in his telling, rather than lose the bonus that was keeping Theo in treatment).
suspect_dossiers.secret: TWO layers. (1) He never meant to kill her; he believed float-aide Rosa still covered East and that Agnes's CGM low-alarm would phone the station — both beliefs are evidence-supported, which is what makes 'reckless, not murder' fair. (2) The gut-punch: Agnes wasn't threatening to destroy him. She was trying to SAVE him — to bring him in as a co-whistleblower with protection before Diane scapegoated him. He let his only ally die. His performed grief is also real grief, and his confession, when it comes, is the confession of a man who finally understands what he chose.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: He plays it as the algorithm's fault and his own months of fraud were all stamped under his real super-user ID — so a lazy read says 'if he were hiding a kill he'd have used a fake account, and he didn't even hide the fraud.' The truth flips this: he used the dormant CADENCE_SVC account ONLY on the death-night. The very inconsistency — honest ID for months of fraud, fake ID for one night — is the tell that the death-night was different, panicked, and his.

suspect_dossiers.name: Diane Pruett
suspect_dossiers.role: Regional director; architect of the incentive scheme and of the fall-guy plan. Morally central, legally distinct — guilty of the fraud-incentive and reckless endangerment, NOT the override.
suspect_dossiers.motive: Her own bonus and the acquisition price ride on 'efficiency.' She designed the night-charge retention bonus, pressured Bernard to 'keep the board green' through the buyer walkthrough, and pre-built deniability by being ready to blame 'the night charge and the algorithm' to the state.
suspect_dossiers.secret: She drafted, in the data room, a contingency memo framing any staffing complaint as 'isolated operator error by night charge' — she had chosen Bernard as the scapegoat before Agnes ever died. She did not order the override and does not know exactly what Bernard did; her crime is creating expendability and willful blindness, then planning to let one desperate man take the fall.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: She is the satisfying 'real villain' the player wants to convict for the death — and the game lets you try. But the door-session and audit metadata prove she never touched the schedule and was off-site. Convicting HER of the homicide is the wrong call; the case's moral test is refusing the easy 'evil executive did it' and charging her correctly for the distinct crime she actually committed.

suspect_dossiers.name: Yvette Marsh
suspect_dossiers.role: Overnight aide; Agnes's protégé; the obvious 'overworked aide snapped' decoy. Actually the key corroborating witness.
suspect_dossiers.motive: Publicly, bitterly vocal about mandatory short-staffing; had argued with Agnes that very week — but about Agnes risking herself to fight management, not out of malice. Classic 'burned-out aide with a master key' framing.
suspect_dossiers.secret: She had been secretly recording voice memos to document unsafe staffing for a state complaint — making her Agnes's spiritual co-conspirator, not her enemy. Her memos and badge trail place her in the West Wing with REAL residents the entire window, and one memo timestamps Petrak as 'fine, just turned around, why am I even over here' — which is what proves the West event was INFLATED, not invented. She carries survivor's guilt: she was the aide pulled away.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: Master key + on shift + open conflict with the victim = textbook suspect. Dies to her own evidence: continuous badge trail in West, voice memos with timestamps, and the fact that her recordings are what crack the case for the detective — the decoy hands you the murder weapon's proof.

suspect_dossiers.name: Owen Whitlock
suspect_dossiers.role: Agnes's nephew and sole heir; a data analyst. The 'greedy heir' decoy — subverted into a near-ally.
suspect_dossiers.motive: Inheritance, on paper. He'd been pushing Agnes to leave Glenharrow, calling it 'too expensive,' and resented the bills — the standard heir motive.
suspect_dossiers.secret: He wasn't greedy; he was scared. As a data analyst he'd noticed the response-time pattern from the visitor-feedback portal and family text alerts and had begged Agnes to move out because he thought the place was DANGEROUS — offering to pay himself. He was trying to save her too, from the outside, the same week she was trying to save Bernard from the inside. He didn't know she'd already gone to war.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: Refuted on BOTH mechanics and theme: ANPR/toll cameras and a timestamped hotel folio place him at a conference two states away (his 'visit' that afternoon was a phone call, logged on Agnes's phone), AND his motive collapses on inspection — his texts to Agnes read 'please just let me get you out of there,' not a threat. He inherits; he didn't kill her; he loved her.

suspect_dossiers.name: Dr. Sana Idris
suspect_dossiers.role: Facility physician; signed the 'natural causes' certificate fast. Complicit-in-haste; becomes a reluctant ally.
suspect_dossiers.motive: Pressured to keep the buyout clean, leaned on by Diane to avoid 'unnecessary scrutiny,' she rubber-stamped a natural-causes finding on a frail elderly diabetic — the path of least resistance during an acquisition.
suspect_dossiers.secret: She is ashamed, not homicidal. When Mara pushes, it is Dr. Idris who reluctantly orders the full tox screen (clean) AND pulls the CGM device data and writes the coroner annotation 'decline gradual; death preventable with timely oral glucose' — the evidence that kills the poison/mercy-killing theories and converts survivability from assumption into shown fact. Her arc is finding the nerve to formally reclassify the manner of death from NATURAL to UNDETERMINED, which is the legal trigger that lets Mara open a case and preserve the logs.
suspect_dossiers.red_herring: Looks like part of the cover-up (fast certificate, conflict of interest). She's a flawed ally whose courage to reverse herself is a mid-game gate, not a culprit — distinguishing 'cowardice corrected' from 'complicity in killing.'

clue_map.clue: Room 114 staged exactly as found: pull-cord call button, insulin pen, an UNOPENED bottle of glucose tablets within arm's reach, oxygen concentrator, CGM clip on the dresser, and a half-finished crossword
clue_map.source: LOCATION — Room 114
clue_map.reveals: A survivable medical crisis with no response. The untouched glucose tablets within reach establish she COULD have self-treated had she been alert/helped — first hint the question is not 'what was done to her' but 'why did no one come.' The crossword is Daniel's nightly ritual with her — emotional thread.

clue_map.clue: Coroner's report: cause = untreated hypoglycemic crisis; full tox screen CLEAN; CGM-derived annotation 'glucose decline gradual over ~45 min; death preventable with timely oral glucose or a single bedside check'
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — Coroner's report (unlocked after Lead pushes Dr. Idris in Act 1 gate)
clue_map.reveals: Kills poison and mercy-killing outright (clean tox). CLOSES the medical-determinism hole: survivability is now SHOWN, not assumed — a gradual, reversible decline, not a sudden unsavable arrest. Reframes the whole case to 'engineered absence.'

clue_map.clue: CGM device log (downloaded from her monitor/phone app): a slow glucose slide starting ~11:50pm, crossing the danger line ~12:10am, flatlining toward 12:40am; PLUS a settings entry showing the phone LOW-alarm was muted three weeks earlier
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — Agnes's CGM data / phone (pulled by Dr. Idris)
clue_map.reveals: Confirms gradual survivable decline (corroborates coroner) AND supports Bernard's false belief defense: he expected the CGM alarm to summon help, but Agnes had silenced it — fairness anchor for 'reckless, not premeditated.'

clue_map.clue: Agnes's phone VOICE MEMOS — ICU-habit nightly notes, including one recorded ~11:30pm the death-night: her own voice worrying about Bernard, saying she's 'giving the boy till Monday to do this the right way, with me'; a final memo ~12:08am, slurred and confused (hypoglycemia), trailing off
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE / PEOPLE-adjacent — Agnes's phone (Partner can surface this; Lead may skip it chasing the audit log)
clue_map.reveals: Brings Agnes ALIVE on-screen (present-tense, her voice). Establishes the INVERSION: she was protecting Bernard, not threatening him. The final slurred memo is medically authentic proof of gradual decline. Two-player hook: the Partner often finds this; the Lead must be advised.

clue_map.clue: CareCadence audit log: East Wing acuity forced to GREEN at 11:53pm; Agnes's call-light fired 11:58pm, auto-cleared 12:39am with NO aide badge-in during the window
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — CareCadence audit log (locker)
clue_map.reveals: An empty wing during a survivable crisis: the central anomaly. Sets up the 'who emptied it' question.

clue_map.clue: NFC door-badge logs: both East aides AND float-pool aide Rosa Delgado badge into the WEST wing at ~11:55pm; no one badges into East again until 12:41am
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — NFC badge logs (locker)
clue_map.reveals: The wing was emptied of EVERY available aide, including the float coverage Bernard claims he relied on. Proves total absence and undercuts his 'someone was still near' belief for that night.

clue_map.clue: West Wing incident record + shift-board history: resident Petrak's event was logged GREEN at 11:51, then RE-RANKED to AMBER-FALL at 11:53 — the same minute East went GREEN — by the same session
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — West incident report + LOCATION nurses'-station shift board
clue_map.reveals: FIXED phantom-event hole: the West event was REAL but INFLATED, not invented. The simultaneous, same-session re-rank of East-down/West-up is the engineered swap — a single human action dressed as two algorithmic ones.

clue_map.clue: Yvette's voice memo timestamped 11:56pm in West: 'Petrak's fine, just turned around again, why are all three of us over here?'
clue_map.source: PEOPLE — Yvette Marsh interview (unlocks her memos)
clue_map.reveals: Proves Petrak's acuity was trivial, so the AMBER-FALL ranking was false inflation. Clears Yvette (places her in West with a real resident) and corroborates the engineered swap from a human witness.

clue_map.clue: Audit-log SESSION metadata: the 11:53 East/West changes were NOT made under Bernard's super-user ID but under a dormant shared vendor account, CADENCE_SVC; every OTHER change that night, and months of prior overrides, carry Bernard's real ID
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — audit log metadata (deduction-board examinable)
clue_map.reveals: FIXED self-incrimination hole. The tell is the SWITCH: honest ID for months, fake account for one night = consciousness of guilt about THIS night specifically. This is an earned inference, not a name read off a column.

clue_map.clue: IT/server-closet records: the CADENCE_SVC password was reset from Bernard's nurses'-station workstation 9 days before the death; and the service-account session that night badged through doors whose readers only Bernard physically traversed (his own badge trail mirrors the service session's door path)
clue_map.source: LOCATION — basement server closet + cross-referenced badge logs
clue_map.reveals: Ties the anonymous CADENCE_SVC session to Bernard's physical body in the building — the EARNED identity link. Closes the 'logs are spoofable' objection: it's not one field, it's password-reset provenance + a physical door-path correlation.

clue_map.clue: Rosa Delgado's reassignment record + roster: Rosa was the float-pool aide nominally assigned to cover East overnight, auto-pulled West by the same 11:53 re-rank
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — staffing roster / PEOPLE — Rosa (minor interview)
clue_map.reveals: Supports Bernard's 'I thought East was still covered' false belief (Rosa was his assumed safety net) — the second pillar of the reckless-not-murder fairness. Also widens the human cost: another aide stretched thin.

clue_map.clue: Agnes's paper RESPONSE-TIME NOTEBOOK: weeks of timed delays in her handwriting; final entry 'B.K. — overrides — told him Monday'; interspersed FUTURE plans (a trip, Daniel's birthday) ruling out suicide
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — notebook (passed to the Partner in 2-player)
clue_map.reveals: The whistleblower spine — but reframed by the voice memo to mean 'gave him a deadline to come forward WITH me,' not 'threatened to report him.' Rules out suicide. Becomes the Partner's inherited instrument (theme).

clue_map.clue: Second aide (corroborating witness) overheard the evening Agnes–Bernard exchange: not a shouting threat but Agnes pleading 'come in with me, Bernie, before they hang it on you'
clue_map.source: PEOPLE — corroborating-aide interview
clue_map.reveals: Confirms the timeline AND the inversion: the confrontation was Agnes trying to save Bernard, deepening his guilt and dodging the worn 'whistleblower threatens killer' beat.

clue_map.clue: Diane's data-room laptop: the 'Transition Retention — Night Charge' bonus tied to a labor-efficiency target; a contingency memo pre-framing any complaint as 'isolated operator error by night charge'; plus an SMS thread to Bernard: 'East has to clear AMBER by the weekend close — keep the board green for the walkthrough, I don't care how'
clue_map.source: LOCATION/EVIDENCE — regional director's laptop (data room)
clue_map.reveals: FIXED Diane-fork hole. The bonus = financial motive; the contingency memo = she pre-selected Bernard as scapegoat (the inversion's foundation); the SMS ties her order to THIS push window WITHOUT ever saying 'empty the wing' — fairly supporting 'charge both, for different crimes.'

clue_map.clue: Bernard's locker/phone: a medical-debt collections letter and a pediatric-oncology payment plan for his son Theo; a half-deleted draft text to Agnes reading 'I can't, I'll lose everything — please just wait'
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — Bernard's locker/phone
clue_map.reveals: Personal desperation behind the fraud (humanizes, doesn't excuse). The draft text proves he understood Agnes's offer and chose fear — sealing recklessness and motive.

clue_map.clue: Owen's alibi packet: ANPR/toll-camera hits and a timestamped hotel folio two states away; Agnes's call log showing his 'visit' was actually a phone call; his texts reading 'please let me get you out of there, I'll pay'
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — Owen alibi records / PEOPLE — Owen interview
clue_map.reveals: Refutes the greedy-heir herring on BOTH alibi (out of state) AND motive (he was trying to save her). Theme-carrying refutation.

clue_map.clue: IT cutover schedule: Meridian's migration begins after Monday 9am signing; the door-session ↔ audit correlation is a DERIVED table regenerated nightly with a rolling 7-day retention — the death-night correlation is on its LAST retained day and is rebuilt/overwritten when IT images the DB for Saturday's walkthrough
clue_map.source: EVIDENCE — IT cutover schedule / LOCATION — server closet
clue_map.reveals: FIXED legally-shaky clock.
